title: Intern, Data Scientist - Marketing Technology & AI
description:
Life at Grab

At Grab, every Grabber is guided by The Grab Way, which spells out our mission, how we believe we can achieve it, and our operating principles - the 4Hs: Heart, Hunger, Honour and Humility. These principles guide and help us make decisions as we work to create economic empowerment for the people of Southeast Asia.

Get to Know the Team

The CAMATECH Martech Analytics team is part of Country & Marketing Analytics, partnering with Marketing, Growth, Country Business, Product, Engineering and other Data teams to define key metrics and deliver data-driven solutions for fast-evolving market needs. We focus on scaling Marketing AI (Comms Compass) and MarTech capabilities—across content generation, insights & reporting, content tagging, country pilots and self-serve access—so marketers can run smarter communications at scale. As a Data Scientist Intern in CAMATECH, you will contribute to analytical frameworks, dashboards and models that power Grab's marketing stack and make our AI-driven tools more useful for teams on the ground.

Get to Know the Role

We are looking for a Data Analytics Intern to support marketing technology and AI-driven initiatives across analytics, reporting, and insight generation. In this role, you will work with cross-functional teams to analyze campaign performance, build automated reporting solutions, track adoption of new tools, and generate insights that improve marketing effectiveness and overall user engagement.

You will be onsite in our Petaling Jaya office.

The Critical Tasks You Will Perform
• Support recurring and ad-hoc analysis for marketing initiatives
• Build and maintain dashboards, scorecards, and reporting pipelines
• Analyze campaign performance, user behavior, and content effectiveness to uncover opportunities for improvement
• Track adoption and usage of new tools and workflows, and identify ways to improve engagement
• Support experimentation and evaluation of AI-enabled use-cases in marketing and communications
• Translate business questions into data-driven insights and recommendations
• Document metrics, reporting logic, and analytical frameworks to support scalable self-serve reporting

What Essential Skills You Will Need
• You will need to start the internship in August 2026 with a minimum duration of 3 months.
• Pursuing a degree in Data Science, Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, or a related field
• Proficient in SQL and spreadsheet skills; Python or R is a plus
• Experience with dashboards and data visualization tools
• Interest in marketing analytics, experimentation, and product performance
• Familiarity with AI / machine learning concepts, such as prompt/context engineering, model evaluation, agentic AI etc.
• Curious about how AI can be applied responsibly to improve business decisions and user experiences

Life at Grab

We care about your well-being at Grab, here are some of the benefits we offer our Interns:
• Attractive Monthly Allowance!
• EPF (Employees Provident Fund) or SOCSO (Social Security Organization) where applicable.
• Balancing personal commitments and life's demands are made easier with our FlexWork arrangements such as differentiated hours

What We Stand For At Grab

We are committed to building an inclusive and equitable workplace that provides equal opportunity for Grabbers to grow and perform at their best. We consider all candidates fairly and equally regardless of nationality, ethnicity, race, religion, age, gender, family commitments, physical and mental impairments or disabilities, and other attributes that make them unique.

title: Internship, Data Office
location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
Do you want to work in a high-trust culture where you’ll feel empowered to make decisions that result in impact?

Contribute to a variety of data and business projects and gain insights in the overall operations of the data office through data mining, business monitoring and regular performance tracking/reports.

You'll be responsible for:
• Participate in the maintenance and creation of regular reports and dashboards
• Assist to mine through large data sets to identify anomalies and investigate the root causes of anomalies to ensure that the data sets are delivered in the required quality
• Work closely with supervisor to deliver data requests from key stakeholders such as actuarial, claims, underwriting, policy management team etc.
• Exposure to various key tools such as Microsoft Excel, SAP BO, SQL and Python to ensure up-to-date data and its availability via continuous data enrichment

Important to your success:
• Currently pursuing bachelor’s degree in Actuarial Science, Computer Science, Data Science, Business Analytics, Information Systems/Technologies any related field.
• Eager and willing to learn, attentive to details, problem solving skills, and strong sense of commitment on outcome delivery in a timely manner
• Good communication skills and collaborative attitudes among the team, various business functions and relevant IT teams
• Familiarity with AI tools like ChatGPT or Copilot is an added advantage.

Internship Intake: Mar 26 Onwards

Internship Duration: Min 3 months

Location: Menara Allianz Sentral (Near KL Sentral)

What to expect:
• Application Submission: Begin by submitting your application through our online portal and include your resume.
• Video Recording Screening: After reviewing your application, you may be invited to participate in a video recording screening. This step allows us to learn more about your skills, experiences, and motivations for applying. You will receive instructions on how to complete this recording.
• Shortlisting: Based on the video recordings, we will shortlist candidates who best match the qualifications and requirements of the internship.
• Interview: Shortlisted candidates will be invited for an interview with our team. This is an opportunity for you to discuss your experiences and aspirations in more detail and for us to answer any questions you may have about the internship.
• Final Selection: After the interviews, successful candidates will be notified and offered a position in our internship program.

We appreciate your interest and look forward to potentially welcoming you to our team. Good luck!

description:
About Grab and Our Workplace

Grab is Southeast Asia's leading superapp. From getting your favourite meals delivered to helping you manage your finances and getting around town hassle-free, we've got your back with everything. The Country & Marketing Analytics (CAMA) team helps us by analyzing our operations and marketing plans across countries. This analysis helps decipher the impact on our business, our brand, and our users, driving better outcomes.

Get to Know the Role

Working with the CAMA x Marketplace Pod, you will focus on turning Malaysia's technical supply and marketplace optimizations into scalable regional assets. You will help build AI agents and "skills" that encapsulate local expertise, allowing analysts across other markets to automate and replicate complex workflows. You will gain hands-on experience interpreting supply-side data and learning the technical process of automating analytical tasks into reusable AI tools. Your contribution will establish a long-term foundation for our technical knowledge, ensuring our local innovations become regional standards that any analyst can use to reduce manual effort.

The Critical Tasks You Will Perform
• Partner with the CAMA Marketplace Pod to understand supply-side operations, identify optimization opportunities, and perform data deep-dives to uncover performance insights.
• Build AI agents and "skills" to automate analytical workflows, and help create reports, dashboards, and frameworks that create applicable decisions.
• Implement technical bridges to transform local insights into scalable assets used across multiple markets, while learning the data infrastructure required to support AI solutions.

What Essential Skills You Will Need
• You will need to start the internship in August 2026 with a minimum duration of 3 months.
• Pursuing a degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Engineering, or a quantitative field

title: Intern, Risk Data Analyst
description:
Company Description

About Grab and Our Workplace

Grab is Southeast Asia's leading superapp. From getting your favourite meals delivered to helping you manage your finances and getting around town hassle-free, we've got your back with everything. In Grab, purpose gives us joy and habits build excellence, while harnessing the power of Technology and AI to deliver the mission of driving Southeast Asia forward by economically empowering everyone, with heart, hunger, honour, and humility.

Job Description

Get to Know the Team

The Integrity team is dedicated to safeguarding all Grab users. We ensure user safety, prevent fraud, and help the Grab ecosystem achieve regulatory compliance. This team involves itself in the entire data lifecycle, from analyzing user behavior to developing detection and prevention modules across user onboarding and transaction journeys.

Get to Know the Role

Reporting to an Analytics Manager in the Integrity team, you will implement controls that protect Grab's ecosystem. You will assist with deep-dive analysis on user behaviors, product funnels, and transaction patterns to help identify potential risks and new fraud typologies. Your analytical support will contribute to the design of risk detection strategies, optimization of AI/Machine Learning models, and implementation of crucial risk controls to safeguard a diverse user base.

You will base onsite in our Petaling Jaya office.

The Critical Tasks You Will Perform

• Assist in measuring and analyzing key risk metrics, such as fraud loss, business/product performance, and the performance/effectiveness of existing risk controls across Grab business verticals.
• Support the identification of new fraud and risk patterns by extracting and visualizing data, contributing to the development of data-driven rules and strategies.
• Perform statistical analyses to improve risk detection systems and ensure data integrity.
• Contribute to reporting and data visibility efforts by preparing datasets and dashboards to empower stakeholders (Product, Engineering, Risk Operations) with insights on key risk metrics and evolving attack patterns.
• Support data extractions and preparations required for internal audits and compliance assessments.
• Help design and analyze experiments to determine the effectiveness of new or existing risk controls within the platforms.

Qualifications

What Essential Skills You Will Need

• You will be able to join internship in August 2026 with a minimum duration of 3 months.
• Currently pursuing Bachelor's Degree in Analytics, Statistics, Computer Science, Economics, or an equivalent quantitative field.
• Basic to intermediate proficiency in SQL/data querying.
• Familiarity with data visualization tools (e.g., Power BI, Tableau, or similar).
• Understanding of basic statistical concepts (e.g., hypothesis testing, probability distributions, regression).
• A high degree of curiosity and willingness to learn about risk management, fraud, and compliance in digital ecosystems.

Additional Information

Life at Grab

We care about your well-being at Grab, here are some of the benefits we offer our Interns:

• Attractive Monthly Allowance!
• EPF (Employees Provident Fund) or SOCSO (Social Security Organization) where applicable.
• Balancing personal commitments and life's demands are made easier with our FlexWork arrangements such as differentiated hours

What We Stand For At Grab

We are committed to building an inclusive and equitable workplace that provides equal opportunity for Grabbers to grow and perform at their best. We consider all candidates fairly and equally regardless of nationality, ethnicity, race, religion, age, gender, family commitments, physical and mental impairments or disabilities, and other attributes that make them unique.

title: Data Analyst - Identity Access Management (Internship)
description:
About the job
Job purpose
Are you passionate about technology and looking to kickstart your career in a world-leading insurance company? AXA Group Operations is offering an exciting opportunity for young, dynamic individuals to join our Identity Global Products’ teams (IGP).

As an intern, you will support the management of master data related to identity products and asset inventories, including establishing and maintaining accurate sources of truth through systems like CMDB (Configuration Management Database) and IT Asset Management (ITAM).

Additionally, you will assist in creating and enhancing reports for the authentication platform, enabling better monitoring and analysis of authentication activities tailored to stakeholders' needs.

Main missions
Your responsibilities include:

• Review existing master data related to identity management systems and asset inventories.

• Assist in data cleansing, deduplication, and standardization efforts.

• Support the documentation of data management processes, standards, and sources of truth, including how to maintain accurate hardware and software inventory data via CMDB and ITAM systems.

• Help ensure data security and compliance with relevant policies.

• Collaborate with Global IGP, security, and asset management teams to validate data accuracy and consistency.

• Gather requirements for reporting needs from stakeholders involved in security, finance, local IAM owners, and application owners.

• Customize reports based on stakeholder needs to support different groups’ insights.

• Automate report generation where possible to enable timely insights.

• Assist in creating dashboards or visualizations for easier interpretation of authentication data.

• Document reporting procedures and provide support for report users.

Expected skills & experience
We are looking for someone with the following skills:

Hard Skills:

• Familiarity with data management principles, sources of truth concepts, and asset inventories (e.g., CMDB, ITAM).

• Familiarity with reporting tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau).

• Basic knowledge of SQL or other data querying languages.

• Basic understanding of identity management systems and their data requirements.

• Understanding of authentication mechanisms, security principles, and asset management.

Soft Skills:

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy.

• Good communication skills for interacting with stakeholders.

• Ability to document processes clearly.

• Proactive attitude and willingness to learn.

Background:

• Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's program in Information Technology, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related fields.

Learning Opportunities

• Hands-On Experience with Identity and Asset Data: Understand how various identity management systems, CMDB, and ITAM tools work together to provide accurate sources of truth for user identities and hardware/software assets.

• Data Management Skills: Learn best practices for data cleansing, standardization, and maintaining reliable sources of truth across systems.

• Reporting and Data Visualization: Develop skills in creating tailored reports and dashboards for diverse stakeholder groups using tools like Power BI or ELK.

• Understanding Security and Asset Lifecycle: Gain insights into security policies, asset lifecycle management, and how accurate data supports compliance and operational decision-making.

• Stakeholder Engagement: Learn how to gather requirements from teams such as security, finance, IAM owners, and application owners, and tailor reports accordingly.

• Problem Solving & Analytical Thinking: Enhance your ability to analyze complex data and provide actionable insights.

• Project Management: Gain experience in planning, executing, and documenting tasks within a professional environment.

What we offer
We bring together the expertise, cultural diversity and creativity of over 8,000 employees worldwide and we’re committed to equal opportunities in all aspects of employment (gender, LGBT+, disabled persons, or people of different origins) and to promoting Diversity & Inclusion by creating a work environment where all employees are treated with dignity and respect, and where individual differences are valued.

About the entity
AXA is becoming a sustainable tech-led company and at AXA Group Operations we are one of the major catalysts for this transformation.
We set the tone by triggering and empowering the evolution of our insurance business model through technology and innovation, driving its concrete implementation globally at speed, with a high quality of advisory and execution.
We are present across 17 countries with committed, highly qualified teams. We leverage technology, data, sourcing, security and investment allocation in a global way, but also achieve economies of scale and synergies when necessary.

At AXA Group Operations, we want to be recognized in three fields of action:
• State-of-the-art Data Technology to drive customer experience
• State-of-the-art Procurement & Sourcing to drive efficiency and better manage risks
• High-Performing Global Team for stronger partnerships with AXA entities
About AXA
As a world-leading insurance company, we act for human progress by protecting what matters. With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. Know you can.

The Fulfilment Product Analytics Team advances product innovation with analytics and experimentation best practices. Job Description

We are looking for an enthusiastic and detail-oriented AI Intern to work on text processing and Large Language Model (LLM)-based applications. This role involves building, fine-tuning, and optimizing language models for various natural language processing (NLP) tasks such as text classification, summarization, information extraction, and conversational AI. You will collaborate with our AI research and engineering teams to develop cutting-edge solutions in the NLP domain.

ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Assist in the design and development of NLP pipelines for tasks like entity recognition, summarization, sentiment analysis, and question answering.
• Preprocess and clean text data for training and evaluation of LLMs.
• Experiment with state-of-the-art LLM frameworks (e.g., Hugging Face Transformers, OpenAI API, LangChain) to solve real-world text processing problems.
• Support fine-tuning of pre-trained models on domain-specific datasets using techniques like LoRA, PEFT, or full model training.
• Develop and maintain prompt engineering strategies for improving model performance.
• Research and implement techniques for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and context optimization.
• Document experiments and present findings to the team.

Qualifications

REQUIRED:
• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI, Machine Learning, or related field.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
• Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

SKILLS:
• Strong foundation in Python (or R) and familiarity with libraries like NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
• Basic understanding of machine learning algorithms (regression, classification, clustering, etc.).
• Familiarity with SQL and databases for data extraction and manipulation.

title: Internship - AI and Data Analytics
description:
Company Description
Sandisk understands how people and businesses consume data and we relentlessly innovate to deliver solutions that enable today's needs and tomorrow's next big ideas. With a rich history of groundbreaking innovations in Flash and advanced memory technologies, our solutions have become the beating heart of the digital world we're living in and that we have the power to shape.
Sandisk meets people and businesses at the intersection of their aspirations and the moment, enabling them to keep moving and pushing possibility forward. We do this through the balance of our powerhouse manufacturing capabilities and our industry-leading portfolio of products that are recognized globally for innovation, performance and quality.
Sandisk has two facilities recognized by the World Economic Forum as part of the Global Lighthouse Network for advanced 4IR innovations. These facilities were also recognized as Sustainability Lighthouses for breakthroughs in efficient operations. With our global reach, we ensure the global supply chain has access to the Flash memory it needs to keep our world moving forward.
Job Description
We are looking for an enthusiastic and detail-oriented AI Intern to work on text processing and Large Language Model (LLM)-based applications. This role involves building, fine-tuning, and optimizing language models for various natural language processing (NLP) tasks such as text classification, summarization, information extraction, and conversational AI. You will collaborate with our AI research and engineering teams to develop cutting-edge solutions in the NLP domain.
Essential Duties And Responsibilities
• Assist in the design and development of NLP pipelines for tasks like entity recognition, summarization, sentiment analysis, and question answering.
• Preprocess and clean text data for training and evaluation of LLMs.
• Experiment with state-of-the-art LLM frameworks (e.g., Hugging Face Transformers, OpenAI API, LangChain) to solve real-world text processing problems.
• Support fine-tuning of pre-trained models on domain-specific datasets using techniques like LoRA, PEFT, or full model training.
• Develop and maintain prompt engineering strategies for improving model performance.
• Research and implement techniques for RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) and context optimization.
• Document experiments and present findings to the team.

Qualifications
REQUIRED:
• Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI, Machine Learning, or related field.
• Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with attention to detail.
• Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

Skills
• Strong foundation in Python (or R) and familiarity with libraries like NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.
• Basic understanding of machine learning algorithms (regression, classification, clustering, etc.).
• Familiarity with SQL and databases for data extraction and manipulation.

Additional Information
Sandisk thrives on the power and potential of diversity. As a global company, we believe the most effective way to embrace the diversity of our customers and communities is to mirror it from within. We believe the fusion of various perspectives results in the best outcomes for our employees, our company, our customers, and the world around us. We are committed to an inclusive environment where every individual can thrive through a sense of belonging, respect and contribution.

title: EY Graduate Programme (Data and Analytics – Financial Services)
location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
Job description

We are the only professional services organisation who has a separate business dedicated exclusively to the financial services marketplace. Join Financial Services (FSO) and you will work with multi-disciplinary teams from around the world to deliver a global perspective. Aligned to key industry groups including asset management, banking and capital markets, insurance and private equity, we provide integrated consulting, assurance, tax, and strategy, transaction services.

At EY, we want you to be yourself. We want you to bring your intelligence, energy and integrity into our business. Think how much further you’ll go surrounded by people who will encourage you to express your ideas and who will value your opinions.

Starting a career with EY is your opportunity to receive early responsibility, support and training as you set out to achieve your career ambitions. You will receive exposure to some of our largest clients and projects, from entrepreneurial start-ups to multinationals, both locally and globally. You’ll also receive guidance on how to help them manage risks, address issues and capitalise on opportunities.

The opportunity

You will be working with our high-profile clients within global banking, capital markets, asset management and insurance sectors, you’ll have the opportunity to affect meaningful, impactful change for Malaysia and Malaysians. We’re building a core team of practitioners - with skills ranging from data architecture, information management, analytics, data science, software engineering, DevOps, to Agile delivery (and much more) - to shape the future of EY’s DnA capability in Malaysia and, with it, to redefine the role that data and analytics will play in solving real problems that effect everyday Malaysians.

At EY, we believe your career is a journey and we are committed to providing an array of exciting opportunities to help you find the career path that is right for you.

Your key responsibilities

·
Be part of a culture that focuses on delivering excellence for our clients and work with an awesome team of people with access to cutting edge technologies.

·
Flexible to work in the way that brings out the best in you and encourages you to do what you love, in an environment that supports you.

·
Involve in all aspects of the Project life-cycle, including Strategy, Road-Mapping, Architecture, Implementation and Development, to gain maximum exposure and set you up for a successful consulting career.

·
Collaborates with analytics and business teams to improve data models that feed business intelligence tools, increasing data accessibility and fostering data-driven decision making across the organization.

·
Implements processes and systems to monitor data quality, ensuring production data is always accurate and available for key stakeholders and business processes that depend on it.

·
Performs data analysis required to troubleshoot data related issues and assist in the resolution of data issues.

·
Works closely with a team of frontend and backend engineers, product managers, and analysts.

Skills and attributes for success
·
A team player with strong communication and interpersonal skills

·
Proactive, dedicated, innovative, resourceful, analytical and able to work under pressure

·
Good command of spoken and written English

·
Strong drive to excel professionally

·
Strong leadership qualities and a good record of extra-curricular activities

·
Knowledge or hands-on experience with some analytic tools

To qualify for the role you must have
·
At least an average distinction/ 2nd upper degree in Data Analytics, Computer Science, Information Technology, Business Analytics or other related fields

·
Currently in your final year or a fresh graduate

·
Individuals with post-graduate degrees and / or professional qualifications are encouraged to apply

You are required to include a cover letter, most updated resume, academic transcripts (secondary & tertiary), certificates, testimonials and other relevant supporting documents with your application to be considered.

Ideally, you’ll also have
·
Past relevant trainee/internship experiences with both technical and non-technical stakeholders

·
You will have demonstrative knowledge or hands-on experience with some of the following;

·
Analytics – SAS, R, Hadoop, Hive

·
BI / Data Visualisation – PowerBI, Tableau, Qlikview,

What we look for

What’s most important is that you’re dedicated to working with your colleagues as part of a high-performing team. You’ll need to thrive in picking up new skills as you go, so natural curiosity, a lot of questions and the confidence to speak up when you see something that could be improved are essential. If you’re ready to take on a wide range of responsibilities, and are committed to seeking out new ways to make a difference, this role is for you.

What working at EY offers

EY offers a competitive remuneration package commensurate with your work experience, where you will be rewarded for your individual and team performance. We are committed to being an inclusive employer and are happy to consider flexible working arrangements (FWA), as needed, guided by our FWA Policy. Plus, we offer:

·
Support, coaching and feedback from engaging colleagues

·
Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career

·
The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that is right for you

About EY

As a global leader in assurance, tax, strategy, transaction and consulting services, we hire and develop the most passionate people in their field to help build a better working world. That starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities and creative freedom to make things better. So that whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime.

If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

Make your mark. Apply now.

title: Business Analyst Internship (6 months)
description:
Business Analyst Internship (6 months)

Location: Shah Alam

Apply now

What you can expect

Interns will be assigned to the Business Analysis department.

► Establishment and formulation of performance indicators

► Creation of visual representations of complex data models for relevant management functions

► Analysis of trends and patterns within data to formulate actionable recommendations

► Opportunity to engage in collaboration with internal stakeholders as part of continuous improvement initiatives

What you will bring

► We're excited to welcome candidates with a Bachelor's degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or any related fields in Data Management!

► If you have some knowledge of SQL, Power-BI, or Power Query, that's a fantastic bonus!

► Don't worry, we'll provide an internship allowance to support you during your time with us.

► We're looking for someone who can start from May 2026 onwards, with a commitment of at least 6 months.

What we offer

What's in it for you?

As an intern with us, you will enjoy a well-rounded internship experience that goes beyond routine tasks:
• Travel Allowance
Support provided to ease your daily commute, so you can focus on learning and contributing.
• Competitive Monthly Allowance
A monthly allowance that recognises your contribution and commitment throughout the internship period.
• Annual Leave & Sick Leave Entitlement
We value well-being and balance, even at intern level.
• Hands-On Experience in the Oil & Gas Industry
Gain real exposure to the oil & gas sector by working on meaningful projects, engaging with professionals, and understanding industry-level operations ,not just theory.
• Practical Learning with Real Business Impact
Apply your academic knowledge in a professional environment, work closely with operational stakeholders, and build skills that will strengthen your future career.

Who we are
The ROSEN Group is a leading global provider of cutting-edge solutions in all areas of the integrity process chain. Since its beginnings as a one-man business in 1981, ROSEN has grown rapidly and is today a technology group that operates in more than 110 countries with over 4,000 highly qualified employees.

ROSEN's products and services:
• Inspection of critical industrial assets to ensure reliable operations of the highest standards and effectiveness
• Customized engineering consultancy providing efficient asset integrity management
• Production and supply of customized novel products and systems
• Market-driven, topical state-of-the-art research and development providing "added-value" products and services

For more information about the ROSEN Group, go to www.rosen-group.com .

Do you have any questions?

Anna Song

Today’s exceptional challenges require your unique skills. Together, we can build the future of data storage.

title: Security Operations Analyst (Internship) - SOC
description:
About the job

Job purpose

Are you passionate about technology and looking to kickstart your career in a world-leading insurance company?

AXA Group Operations is offering an exciting opportunity for young, dynamic individuals to join us and gain exposure in SOC (Security Operations Center).

Main missions

Your responsibilities include:
• Develop and maintain an LLM based chatbot to help security analysts investigate real Cybersecurity incidents
• Design AI agent architectures to classify and enrich security incident data
• Use and maintain complex enterprise grade CI/CD pipelines
• Keep up with AI research and innovation autonomously

Expected skills & experience

We are looking for someone with the following skills:

Background:
• Bachelor's in Computer Science with a speciality in Cybersecurity, DevOps or AI
• GitHub or personal projects in the relevant fields as well as hackathon/CTF participation strongly appreciated

Hard Skills:
• Python mandatory. Knowledge of libraries and frameworks like Langchain, FastAPI, pandas will be greatly appreciated
• Containerization technology like Docker, Kubernetes, OpenShift appreciated
• Cybersecurity knowledge and secure development practices appreciated
• Frontend and backend development as well as database skills (postgres) appreciated
• CI/CD practices also appreciated
• Experience of Azure/OpenShift appreciated

Soft Skills:
• Autonomy, proactivity, can do attitude
• Communication skills and the ability to present information synthetically
• Ability to learn on the job and adapt to new technologies
• Giving and receiving feedback

Learning opportunities
• Be at the bleeding edge of LLM applications
• Learn the full lifecycle of cybersecurity incident management

What we offer

We bring together the expertise, cultural diversity and creativity of over 8,000 employees worldwide and we’re committed to equal opportunities in all aspects of employment (gender, LGBT+, disabled persons, or people of different origins) and to promoting Diversity & Inclusion by creating a work environment where all employees are treated with dignity and respect, and where individual differences are valued.

About the entity

AXA is becoming a sustainable tech-led company and at AXA Group Operations we are one of the major catalysts for this transformation.

We set the tone by triggering and empowering the evolution of our insurance business model through technology and innovation, driving its concrete implementation globally at speed, with a high quality of advisory and execution.

We are present across 17 countries with committed, highly qualified teams. We leverage technology, data, sourcing, security and investment allocation in a global way, but also achieve economies of scale and synergies when necessary.

At AXA Group Operations, we want to be recognized in three fields of action:
• State-of-the-art Data Technology to drive customer experience
• State-of-the-art Procurement & Sourcing to drive efficiency and better manage risks
• High-Performing Global Team for stronger partnerships with AXA entities

About AXA

As a world-leading insurance company, we act for human progress by protecting what matters. With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. Know you can.

title: EY Graduate Programme - Technology Consulting (Technology Solution Delivery Associate, Financial Services)
location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
Job description

We are the only professional services organization who has a separate business dedicated exclusively to the financial services marketplace. Join Financial Services (FSO) and you will work with multi-disciplinary teams from around the world to deliver a global perspective. Aligned to key industry groups including asset management, banking and capital markets, insurance and private equity, we provide integrated consulting, assurance, tax, and strategy, transaction services.

Starting a career with EY is your opportunity to receive early responsibility, support and training as you set out to achieve your career ambitions. You will receive exposure to some of our largest clients and projects, from entrepreneurial start-ups to multinationals, both locally and globally. You’ll also receive guidance on how to help them manage risks, address issues and capitalise on opportunities.

The opportunity

EY Consulting has exciting opportunities for highly motivated graduates to join our team in Technology Solution Delivery dedicated to the financial services marketplace. In this role, you will have the opportunity to team with a variety of clients to deliver professional services and to actively participate in a rapidly growing practice, while being provided learning opportunities and coaching to develop core consulting competencies.

You will be part of the team in the execution of clients’ assignments in the areas of:

· Information management and business intelligence
· Digital strategy, architecture and solution design (including package solutions)
· Technology operational effectiveness and business case development
· Program and project management
· Information Technology Architecture
At EY, we believe your career is a journey and we are committed to providing an array of exciting opportunities to help you find the career path that is right for you.

Your key responsibilities
· Work in high-performing teams that deliver exceptional client service, from responding to regulatory reforms to managing capital life cycle and improving business operations – playing your part in building a better working world
· Effectively contribute in performance improvement projects, develop analysis, engage client teams and stakeholders, and consistently deliver high-quality work deliverables
· Establish relationships with client personnel at appropriate levels
· Monitor progress, manage risk and ensure key stakeholders are kept informed about progress and expected outcomes
· Stay abreast of current business and industry trends relevant to the client's business, actively assess and present ways to best serve clients, with a focus to develop and maintain long-term client relationships and networks
· Demonstrate in-depth technical capabilities, professional knowledge and ability to assimilate new knowledge
· Develop in-depth understanding of EY and its service lines, remain current on new developments in consulting services capabilities and build relationships with team members across all EY practices to serve clients’ needs
Skills and attributes for success
· A team player with strong communication and interpersonal skills
· Proactive, dedicated, innovative, resourceful, analytical and able to work under pressure
· Good command of spoken and written English
· Strong drive to excel professionally
· Strong leadership qualities and a good record of extra-curricular activities
To qualify for the role you must have
· At least an average distinction/ 2nd upper degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, Information Technology, Computer Engineering, IT Security, Management in Information Systems or other related fields
· Currently in your final year or a fresh graduate
· Individuals with post-graduate degrees and / or professional qualifications are encouraged to apply
You are required to include a cover letter, most updated resume, academic transcripts (secondary & tertiary), certificates, testimonials and other relevant supporting documents with your application to be considered.

Ideally, you’ll also have
· Past relevant trainee/internship experiences
What we look for

What’s most important is that you’re dedicated to working with your colleagues as part of a high-performing team. You’ll need to thrive in picking up new skills as you go, so natural curiosity, a lot of questions and the confidence to speak up when you see something that could be improved are essential. If you’re ready to take on a wide range of responsibilities, and are committed to seeking out new ways to make a difference, this role is for you.

What working at EY offers

EY offers a competitive remuneration package commensurate with your work experience, where you will be rewarded for your individual and team performance. We are committed to being an inclusive employer and are happy to consider flexible working arrangements (FWA), as needed, guided by our FWA Policy. Plus, we offer:

· Support, coaching and feedback from engaging colleagues
· Opportunities to develop new skills and progress your career
· The freedom and flexibility to handle your role in a way that is right for you
About EY

As a global leader in assurance, tax, strategy, transaction and consulting services, we hire and develop the most passionate people in their field to help build a better working world. That starts with a culture that believes in giving you the training, opportunities and creative freedom to make things better. So that whenever you join, however long you stay, the exceptional EY experience lasts a lifetime.

If you can confidently demonstrate that you meet the criteria above, please contact us as soon as possible.

Make your mark. Apply now.

Want to get to know us better?
Visit www.ey.com/my/careers
Become a fan on Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/EYMalaysiaCareers

Connect with us on Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/eymycareers

description:
About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth.
WPP Media is WPP's AI-driven media operating unit, bringing together media, data, and partnerships to deliver creative personalisation at scale. Connected through WPP Open and powered by Open Intelligence, clients see exactly where, how, and why their media investment is working.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

Analytics Intern (Bachelors) — Analytics Delivery Team (Malaysia Data Hub)

Company: WPP Media

Team: Analytics Delivery Team — Malaysia Data Hub
Location: Malaysia (Hybrid/Onsite as per business need)

Internship Type: Internship (Full-time preferred)

Duration: Minimum 5–6 months

About the Role

WPP Media’s Analytics Delivery Team (Malaysia Data Hub) is looking for a Masters-level Analytics Intern to support reporting, dashboarding, and insight generation for business and media stakeholders. This is a hands-on role where you will build and improve Power BI dashboards, write SQL queries for data extraction/validation, and automate workflows using Python.
Key Responsibilities

• Build and enhance Power BI dashboards (DAX measures, data modeling, visuals, drill-throughs, performance optimization)

• Write and optimize SQL queries to extract, validate, and transform data from multiple sources

• Support analytics delivery by converting business needs into clear KPIs, reporting views, and insights

• Use Python to automate data preparation, reporting workflows, and analysis tasks

• Assist with data quality checks, reconciliation, and anomaly detection

• Maintain documentation for dashboards, KPI logic, data definitions, and solution workflows

• Collaborate with analysts, data engineers, and stakeholders in an agile delivery environment

Required Skills (Must-have)

• Strong Power BI experience (Power Query, DAX, data modeling, best-practice visual design)

• Strong SQL skills (joins, aggregations, window functions, troubleshooting data issues)

• Development of analytical, technical, and decision-support skills.

• Truck Transportation

This is a structured 4 to 12-month internship designed to give you real-world experience in AI-assisted B2B outreach and GTM campaign execution.

You will not be doing “theory-based training.” You will work on live client campaigns under supervision and be guided with clear performance metrics.

Data Collection & Data Quality Assurance

• Research and identify relevant companies and decision-makers using AI-assisted tools

• Execute personalised, value-driven outreach via email, LinkedIn, and digital channels for data collection (get & check)

Data Analyst for Sales & Performance Tracking

• Learn how GTM strategies are executed in real environments through hands-on work.

• Track outreach performance, response rates, and meeting conversions.

CRM & Data Hygiene

• Maintain accurate records of data collection outreach activities and prospect interactions.

• Update CRM systems consistently to ensure data quality and visibility.

• Review results regularly and refine outreach approaches based on feedback.

Who We’re Looking For

This role is suitable for students or fresh graduates who are:

• Curious about Data, AI, tech sales, or startups

• Strong communicators

• Proactive and disciplined

• Interested in exploring Data, GTM, digital outreach, and revenue operations

You may be a good fit if you:

• Communicate confidently and professionally in English and Bahasa Melayu

• Are comfortable initiating conversations and following up

• Are resilient, disciplined, and motivated by measurable goals

• Want to build a career in Data, AI, tech sales, or future entrepreneurship

What You Will Gain:

• Hands-on startup exposure

• Practical experience with Data and AI-assisted GTM tools

• Real B2B outreach experience for foundation on Data collection & Data analytics

• Structured mentorship from founders

• Exposure to modern sales systems and revenue operations

High-performing interns may be considered for full-time conversion opportunities upon completion of the internship (subject to performance and business needs).

Leadership & Mentorship

You will work closely with the founding team led by Elton Lee (ex-Google, Microsoft, Oracle), bringing real-world enterprise and startup sales experience across Asia, including AI-led GTM initiatives.

Coaching is practical, execution-focused, and based on live work — not theory.

4 day work from office, 1 day work from home every week.

Free basic breakfast.

Incorporated in 1969, Carlsberg Brewery Malaysia Berhad is part of the global Carlsberg Group — one of the world’s leading brewers with strong market presence across Europe and Asia. What You’ll Be Doing

• Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and data analysis tools.
• A team player with positive attitude and initiative to drive continuous improvement.
• Strong organizational and time management skills with the ability to meet deadlines.

What You Can Expect

An organisation that invests in your growth and empowers you to excel.

We consciously create an environment that genuinely focuses on developing our people—through coaching, continuous an organisation that invests in your growth and empowers you to excel

We empower individuals to take ownership, make decisions, and learn from both successes and challenges. In line with our Growth Culture, we are committed to supporting you in reaching your full potential while creating meaningful impact.

A positive, inclusive, and high energy workplace where your contributions matter. The role places you at the heart of consumer experiences, and you will be supported by a team that values collaboration, compassion, and diverse perspectives.

You can expect a team that celebrates wins together, moves with speed, and embraces innovation to deliver extraordinary results. Your ideas, courage to challenge the status quo, and passion for our consumer will be recognized and appreciated.

title: Security Defense Analyst (Intern)
description:
Company Description

Clone & Freemen has been a trusted technology partner for over 28 years, specializing in creating integrated and innovative solutions for B2B leaders. From managed IT and cybersecurity to digital design and IoT systems, the company delivers tailored services through its MSP+ ecosystem. Operating as a full-service partner, Clone & Freemen bridges the gap between maintaining operational stability and driving forward-looking technological innovation. Their mission is to empower businesses with seamless, connected technology solutions that support both current operations and future growth. The company is known for its commitment to accountability and delivering impactful results.

The Role

As a Security Defense Analyst, you are the first line of defense for our security posture, operating in a 7x24 shift environment to ensure continuous monitoring and response. You will monitor security alerts, investigate potential threats, and respond to incidents in our hybrid environment—covering on‑premises systems, cloud infrastructure (AWS/Azure), and endpoints. You will perform initial triage using Sumo Logic as our primary SIEM, escalate confirmed incidents to senior analysts, and execute routine defense tasks such as log reviews, IOC hunts, and playbook-driven responses. You will work closely with the EDR team to correlate endpoint telemetry with broader security events and ensure rapid containment. This role is the foundation of our Security team, offering broad exposure to modern security tools and a clear path to growing into an incident responder or threat hunter.

Shift expectations:
• Work a rotating schedule 24 hours a day, 7 days a week
• Shifts may include nights, weekends, and public holidays
• Hand off critical context to incoming shift analysts using standardized shift logs
• Maintain alertness and quality of analysis regardless of time of day

Key Responsibilities

1. Security Monitoring & Alert Triage (24/7)

· Monitor Sumo Logic dashboards, scheduled searches, and real-time alerts continuously during your shift

· Assess severity, validate false positives, and escalate confirmed incidents to L2/L3 analysts (or on-call engineers as needed)

· Document initial findings and alert context for efficient handoff to follow‑on shifts

2. Initial Incident Response

· Perform first‑level investigation of suspected malware, unauthorized access, or policy violations

· Execute predefined response actions (e.g., isolate endpoint, revoke sessions, block IOCs)

· Collaborate with the EDR team (available during core hours or via on call) to validate endpoint alerts and coordinate containment steps

3. Log Review & Threat Hunting Support

· Use Sumo Logic queries to review critical logs (authentication, firewall, cloud audit trails) for suspicious patterns

· During quieter shift periods, assist senior analysts in proactive threat hunting using Sumo Logic’s search and analytics features

· Maintain awareness of emerging threats and apply IOCs to the environment using Sumo Logic threat intelligence sources

4. Cloud Security Support

· Monitor cloud security posture (AWS Security Hub, Azure Defender) for misconfigurations

· Correlate cloud logs ingested into Sumo Logic (e.g., AWS CloudTrail, Azure Audit Logs)

· Assist in investigating cloud‑related alerts and support basic remediation under guidance (escalating to the cloud team if needed)

5. Documentation & Handoff

· Document incident timelines, actions taken, and Sumo Logic queries used for future reference

· Contribute to security runbooks and knowledge base articles for common scenarios

· Maintain detailed shift logs and provide a clear, actionable handoff to the next shift analyst

6. Collaboration & Escalation

· Work closely with the EDR team to correlate SIEM alerts with endpoint telemetry and ensure unified incident response

· Partner with EDR engineers (during alignment hours or via async communication) to fine-tune detection rules

· Communicate clearly with internal stakeholders about security status and required actions, even outside normal business hours

· Participate in team meetings, post‑incident reviews, and knowledge‑sharing sessions (as schedule permits)

The role includes comparing solution options, defining requirements with cross-functional teams, and collaborating with business, technical, and sustainability stakeholders to ensure alignment and progress of the DPP initiative.

You will also be working with:

• ERP Solution (E.g: SAP S/4HANA, S/4C)

title: Data Analyst - Identity Access Management (Internship)
description:
About The Job
Job purpose
Are you passionate about technology and looking to kickstart your career in a world-leading insurance company? AXA Group Operations is offering an exciting opportunity for young, dynamic individuals to join our Identity Global Products’ teams (IGP).

As an intern, you will support the management of master data related to identity products and asset inventories, including establishing and maintaining accurate sources of truth through systems like CMDB (Configuration Management Database) and IT Asset Management (ITAM).

Additionally, you will assist in creating and enhancing reports for the authentication platform, enabling better monitoring and analysis of authentication activities tailored to stakeholders' needs.

Main missions
Your Responsibilities Include

• Review existing master data related to identity management systems and asset inventories.

• Assist in data cleansing, deduplication, and standardization efforts.

• Support the documentation of data management processes, standards, and sources of truth, including how to maintain accurate hardware and software inventory data via CMDB and ITAM systems.

• Help ensure data security and compliance with relevant policies.

• Collaborate with Global IGP, security, and asset management teams to validate data accuracy and consistency.

• Gather requirements for reporting needs from stakeholders involved in security, finance, local IAM owners, and application owners.

• Customize reports based on stakeholder needs to support different groups’ insights.

• Automate report generation where possible to enable timely insights.

• Assist in creating dashboards or visualizations for easier interpretation of authentication data.

• Document reporting procedures and provide support for report users.

Expected Skills & Experience
We are looking for someone with the following skills:
Hard Skills

• Familiarity with data management principles, sources of truth concepts, and asset inventories (e.g., CMDB, ITAM).

• Familiarity with reporting tools (e.g., Excel, Power BI, Tableau).

• Basic knowledge of SQL or other data querying languages.

• Basic understanding of identity management systems and their data requirements.

• Understanding of authentication mechanisms, security principles, and asset management.

Soft Skills

• Strong attention to detail and accuracy.

• Good communication skills for interacting with stakeholders.

• Ability to document processes clearly.

• Proactive attitude and willingness to learn.

Background

• Currently enrolled in a Bachelor's program in Information Technology, Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related fields.

Learning Opportunities

• Hands-On Experience with Identity and Asset Data: Understand how various identity management systems, CMDB, and ITAM tools work together to provide accurate sources of truth for user identities and hardware/software assets.

• Data Management Skills: Learn best practices for data cleansing, standardization, and maintaining reliable sources of truth across systems.

• Reporting and Data Visualization: Develop skills in creating tailored reports and dashboards for diverse stakeholder groups using tools like Power BI or ELK.

• Understanding Security and Asset Lifecycle: Gain insights into security policies, asset lifecycle management, and how accurate data supports compliance and operational decision-making.

• Stakeholder Engagement: Learn how to gather requirements from teams such as security, finance, IAM owners, and application owners, and tailor reports accordingly.

• Problem Solving & Analytical Thinking: Enhance your ability to analyze complex data and provide actionable insights.

This internship is designed as a hands-on learning experience, where you will explore automation technologies, support ongoing initiatives, and contribute fresh ideas to improve how we work.

You will gain exposure to real-world business challenges, automation tools, and emerging AI capabilities, while working alongside experienced Business Analysts, Automation Developers, and Business Partners.

This role is ideal for someone who is curious about technology, enjoys simplifying complex ideas, and is eager to learn how automation and data-driven solutions can create business impact.

You will be part of RSS Business Analytics - a global team with regional presence in Switzerland, India, Costa Rica, Hungary, Latvia and Malaysia. We operate in an agile organizational set-up without extensive hierarchical structure, where all our employees lead from every seat. Inspirational leadership, service oriented behavior and entrepreneurial customer centric mindset are our core values.

As part of your 6-month journey, you will have the chance to dive into one or more of these core areas:

• Hands-on Development: Work on internal automation projects using UiPath, Python, or scripting to streamline our own team's workflows.

• MS migration analysis: Research our upcoming move to Microsoft 365, evaluating the effects on existing automations and proposing best practices for a smooth bot migration.

• Solution Strategy & Showcase: Develop an "Automation Toolkit" (decision trees and tool matrices) to match business problems with the right tech (AI vs. RPA). You’ll then bring this to life by curating a digital "Solution Catalogue" to showcase our team’s capabilities and toolkit.

• Ad-hoc Exploration: Stay agile! You’ll support various team initiatives as they arise, giving you a 360-degree view of Business Analytics.

Require:

• Basic understanding of programming or scripting concepts

• Familiarity with any language such as Python, JavaScript, or similar (academic or personal projects is sufficient)

• Strong curiosity and willingness to learn new technologies

• Ability to break down complex ideas into simple, structured formats

• Good communication and presentation skills (written and verbal in English)

Nice to have (not mandatory)

• Exposure to automation tools (e.g. UiPath, Power Automate, Google Apps Script)

• Experience creating presentations, documentation, or visual content

What we offer:

• Mentorship: Regular 1:1s with experienced Data Scientists and Automation Developers.

• Real Impact: Your projects (like the Tool Matrix or Solution Catalogue) will be used by the team long after your internship ends.

• Global Exposure: Experience how a leading healthcare company uses technology to impact patients' lives.

• Flexible Environment: A culture that values "leading from every seat" and prioritizes your development over a rigid job title.

JOB SUMMARY

Data science interns assist data scientists in collecting, analyzing, and interpret data sets to drive optimization and improvement of product development. As part of the company's trainees, data science interns are required to provide help in developing custom data models and algorithms to apply to data sets. Data science interns must also coordinate with different functional teams to implement models and monitor outcomes.
ESSENTIAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

EDUCATIONAL BACKGROUND & EXPERIENCE
• Students who are taking his/her Bachelor or above degree in Computer Science, Machine Learning, Mathematics or Statistics

title: IT Audit Analyst (Internship)
description:
About the job

Job purpose

Are you passionate about technology and looking to kickstart your career in a world-leading insurance company?

AXA Group Operations is offering an exciting opportunity for young, dynamic individuals to join our AXA Asia Internal Audit team.

Main missions

Your responsibilities include:
• Support IT audit team members in conducting audit control testing and documentation, under the guidance of Senior IT Auditors.
• Partner with Internal Audit team members to provide recommendations to management for strengthening controls and work with management to develop acceptable solutions to mitigate risk.
• Perform any other ad-hoc duties as assigned by supervisors.

Expected skills & experience

We are looking for someone with the following experience and skills:

Background:
• University students in information management system, information security, Computer Science or other related disciplines preferable with desire to pursue a career in IT Audit.

Hard Skills:
• Good command of both written and spoken English
• Strong computer skills required, well versed in Microsoft Office and Excel
• Data analytic skills (e.g. PowerBI, Tableau, etc.), Artificial Intelligence and Cloud computing experience are preferred but not mandatory

Soft Skills:
• Good written and verbal communication and presentation skills
• Excellent team player, passionate, committed, and eager to succeed
• Self -starters with good analytical and problem-solving skills with strong attention to details.
• Able to collaborate with senior staff, raise issues and ask questions

Learning opportunities
• Involved in various stages of audit engagements and gain comprehensive insight and experience into the complete Internal IT Audit process.
• Broaden your horizons by working on highly integrated teams across the globe and collaborate with people of diverse backgrounds — both professionally and culturally

What we offer

We bring together the expertise, cultural diversity and creativity of over 8,000 employees worldwide and we’re committed to equal opportunities in all aspects of employment (gender, LGBT+, disabled persons, or people of different origins) and to promoting Diversity & Inclusion by creating a work environment where all employees are treated with dignity and respect, and where individual differences are valued.

About the entity

AXA is becoming a sustainable tech-led company and at AXA Group Operations we are one of the major catalysts for this transformation.

We set the tone by triggering and empowering the evolution of our insurance business model through technology and innovation, driving its concrete implementation globally at speed, with a high quality of advisory and execution.

We are present across 17 countries with committed, highly qualified teams. We leverage technology, data, sourcing, security and investment allocation in a global way, but also achieve economies of scale and synergies when necessary.

At AXA Group Operations, we want to be recognized in three fields of action:
• State-of-the-art Data Technology to drive customer experience
• State-of-the-art Procurement & Sourcing to drive efficiency and better manage risks
• High-Performing Global Team for stronger partnerships with AXA entities

About AXA

As a world-leading insurance company, we act for human progress by protecting what matters. With 153,000 employees in 54 countries working for 105 million customers, we’ve created a truly dynamic and vibrant community. Inclusion and diversity link closely with our values, and together we’re nurturing a culture of respect, for each other, for our customers and the communities around us. Join AXA and you’ll feel like you belong, are included and can thrive. You’ll be able to shape the way you work and truly grow your potential as you seek out new opportunities, push boundaries and benefit people in critical moments of their lives. This is your chance to build the tomorrow you want. Know you can.

description:
Join Sandisk as an AI Intern to develop and optimize NLP and Large Language Model applications in a leading global memory technology company.

Your Role

• Assist in designing and developing NLP pipelines for entity recognition, summarization, sentiment analysis, and question answering.

• Preprocess and clean text data for training and evaluation of Large Language Models (LLMs).

• Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Computer Science, Data Science, AI, Machine Learning, or related field.

• Excellent communication and teamwork skills.

• Strong foundation in Python (or R) and familiarity with libraries such as NumPy, Pandas, Scikit-learn, TensorFlow, or PyTorch.

• Basic understanding of machine learning algorithms including regression, classification, and clustering.

• Familiarity with SQL and databases for data extraction and manipulation.

title: Internship - Automation Developer
description:
At Roche you can show up as yourself, embraced for the unique qualities you bring. Our culture encourages personal expression, open dialogue, and genuine connections, where you are valued, accepted and respected for who you are, allowing you to thrive both personally and professionally. This is how we aim to prevent, stop and cure diseases and ensure everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Join Roche, where every voice matters.

The Position

As an Automation Intern, you will be part of our Data Science & Automation (D&A) team within the Business Analytics function. This internship is designed as a hands-on learning experience, where you will explore automation technologies, support ongoing initiatives, and contribute fresh ideas to improve how we work.

You will gain exposure to real-world business challenges, automation tools, and emerging AI capabilities, while working alongside experienced Business Analysts, Automation Developers, and Business Partners.

This role is ideal for someone who is curious about technology, enjoys simplifying complex ideas, and is eager to learn how automation and data-driven solutions can create business impact.

You will be part of RSS Business Analytics - a global team with regional presence in Switzerland, India, Costa Rica, Hungary, Latvia and Malaysia. We operate in an agile organizational set-up without extensive hierarchical structure, where all our employees lead from every seat. Inspirational leadership, service oriented behavior and entrepreneurial customer centric mindset are our core values.

As part of your 6-month journey, you will have the chance to dive into one or more of these core areas:

• Hands-on Development: Work on internal automation projects using UiPath, Python, or scripting to streamline our own team's workflows.

• MS migration analysis: Research our upcoming move to Microsoft 365, evaluating the effects on existing automations and proposing best practices for a smooth bot migration.

• Solution Strategy & Showcase: Develop an "Automation Toolkit" (decision trees and tool matrices) to match business problems with the right tech (AI vs. RPA). You’ll then bring this to life by curating a digital "Solution Catalogue" to showcase our team’s capabilities and toolkit.

• Ad-hoc Exploration: Stay agile! You’ll support various team initiatives as they arise, giving you a 360-degree view of Business Analytics.

Require

• Basic understanding of programming or scripting concepts

• Familiarity with any language such as Python, JavaScript, or similar (academic or personal projects is sufficient)

• Strong curiosity and willingness to learn new technologies

• Ability to break down complex ideas into simple, structured formats

• Good communication and presentation skills (written and verbal in English)

Nice to have (not mandatory)

• Exposure to automation tools (e.g. UiPath, Power Automate, Google Apps Script)

• Experience creating presentations, documentation, or visual content

What we offer

• Mentorship: Regular 1:1s with experienced Data Scientists and Automation Developers.

• Real Impact: Your projects (like the Tool Matrix or Solution Catalogue) will be used by the team long after your internship ends.

• Global Exposure: Experience how a leading healthcare company uses technology to impact patients' lives.

• Flexible Environment: A culture that values "leading from every seat" and prioritizes your development over a rigid job title.

Who we are A healthier future drives us to innovate. Together, more than 100’000 employees across the globe are dedicated to advance science, ensuring everyone has access to healthcare today and for generations to come. Our efforts result in more than 26 million people treated with our medicines and over 30 billion tests conducted using our Diagnostics products.

We empower each other to explore new possibilities, foster creativity, and keep our ambitions high, so we can deliver life-changing healthcare solutions that make a global impact.

Let’s build a healthier future, together.

Roche is an Equal Opportunity Employer.

title: Business Analyst — Custom Solutions Delivery
location: Kuala Lumpur, Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur
description:
You'll sit between a customer who knows their business and a developer who knows how to build software, and you'll be the person who makes sure they end up with the same picture in their head. That means running workshops with a bank's operations team, a pharma QA lead, or an O&G procurement head — understanding their process well enough to challenge it when they're asking for the wrong thing — and turning the agreed solution into user stories, process flows, and functional specs our developers can build from without coming back to ask "what did the customer actually mean?"

You will not be a note-taker. You will not be a meeting scheduler. You will be the person who owns "is this what the customer needs and is this what we're actually building?" — from kick-off through go-live.

What we need you to already know

At least three-years as a BA on custom software delivery projects — not internal IT, not pure process consulting, not "I helped configure Office 365 at my last job." You've written user stories with acceptance criteria that developers actually built from. You've run requirements workshops with non-technical business users and walked out with structure. You can map a business process clearly (flowchart, whatever — the format matters less than the thinking).

You have working knowledge of the Microsoft ecosystem — SharePoint, PowerApps, Power BI, Azure — enough to know roughly when each is the right answer, or enough to know what to ask. SQL literacy and a conceptual grip on APIs are a plus, not a must.

If your last role was end-user support or pure documentation, this isn't your next step.

What we need you to be

Someone who can lead a requirements workshop with a customer's department head and three of their team leads — set the agenda, ask the questions that surface what people aren't saying, push back on assumptions without losing the room, and walk everyone to a decision before the meeting ends. Someone who writes the spec afterward with the same clarity they brought to the discussion, because the workshop only matters if the documentation is right. Someone calm with senior customers without being deferential to them — client-focused, not client-controlled.

Someone who, when UAT surfaces something the spec missed, owns the gap instead of pointing at the document.

We're a 40-person firm with 40 enterprise customers across banking, insurance, O&G, and pharma. You'll get range, you'll get exposure, and you'll get pushed. If you want a seat where nothing changes for two years, we're not it.

Three things we live by

Shoshin — beginner's mind. We've been doing this 17 years and we still assume the customer knows their business better than we do.

One degree more — the difference between good and excellent is small and compounding. We notice it.

There's always a way — when the answer isn't obvious, we don't escalate, we think harder.

How to apply

Send your CV plus a 150-word answer to this question:

Describe a project where the business user gave you a requirement that, once you understood the underlying problem, turned out to be wrong. What did you discover, what did you recommend instead, and what was the outcome?

Applications without the written response will not be reviewed. We read every one that includes it.

Based at Menara SuezCap, Bangsar South (direct LRT). Malaysian candidates, KL/Selangor.

title: Associate Business Analyst - DAC
description:
Organization The DHL Express team comprises over 110,000 people in practically every country on the planet, and we are part of the DPDHL group. And yet they all have one important thing in common – the customer.

That’s because, although logistics are what drives our world, people are what connect it. Delivering service, quality and sustainability, every single day to every single customer, is how we’re able to bring people together – and make a difference to their lives. As a worldwide market leader in express transportation and logistics, DHL provides solutions for the management and transportation of goods.

We have a global network and local expertise at our disposal. And we are all Certified International Specialists.

The digital automation center (DAC) is the Express Global RPA expertise center, from which high-quality and complex digi services are provided for various DHL business units globally and cross functional. Besides RPA also other digi technologies are being used (ML, NLP, API, Smart OCR, image processing and others. DAC is part of the CEO office and is ideally positioned to create the necessary digi momentum and has also teams respectively based in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia and San Jose, Costa Rica & Maastricht, Netherlands.

Working within DAC is quite exiting as we are active within all functional areas (such as Customer Service, Customs, Network Operations, etc.) within the DHL Express organization and fully focused on digitalization and automation.

Overall role purpose

As a Business Analyst you will be expected to manage (global) deployment projects, as well as to be able to assume the lead in managing suitable project streams/workgroups, taking responsibility for the necessary people, project and performance management aspects. Your analytical and conceptual capabilities meet the challenge posed by complex problems. Your work also involves doing detailed process analyses to monitor digital solutions and to identify gaps where actions would be necessary.
Your business area

You will be a part of our Digital Automation Center and you will be based in Kuala Lumpur.
Main accountabilities: -

Business analysis services

• Responsible for detailed process analysis for endorsed initiatives to assess if digital automation would be a proper solution

• Perform detailed GAP analysis to ensure automation roll out the digital solution is suitable for a country/region

• Use agile data modelling practices to analyze and create suggestions for adequate business solutions. Monitor digital solutions and identify GAPS by preparing technical and/or business reports by collecting, analyzing and summarizing information and trends. Conduct insightful, ad hoc analyses to investigate ongoing or one-time operational issues

Project management

• Overall responsible to support PM in project assessment and project management of deployments

• Set up a standard deployment toolkit to facilitate regional and/or global deployments

• Roll out deployments. Keep deployment heat map up to date.

• Drive testing and acceptance of project results

• Lead streams or (smaller) project teams

• Translate project requirements into project objectives and tasks

• Support administration of project management plans and processes for successful project execution Monitor proactively risks, issues, and scope, ensuring the appropriate level of priority, visibility and escalation

• Obtain feedback at any critical project milestones and at project completion to ensure that projects efforts meets customer expectations

• Utilize project management processes and methodologies at all times

Stakeholder management & Teamwork

• Manage complexity: multiple stakeholders, multiple countries and gain buy-in for project goals

• Address task-related issues appropriately to maintain work relationships

• Contribute to team effort by accomplishing related results as needed

• Explain facts, practices, policies, etc. to others within the organization adhering to the governance model

• Build relationships and understand customer and key stakeholders interests and concerns

• Builds partnerships to collaborate and uses influence to gather Customer and business requirements and communicate effectively with all project Teams and support groups
